title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a weekend trip.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you booked anywhere for our weekend away? || Man: I found a guest house near the beach, but it only has one room left. || Woman: Is it expensive? || Man: Not too bad. The problem is breakfast isn't included. || Woman: That's fine. [8]I'd rather stay close to the sea than save a few pounds and have a long walk every morning[/8]. || Man: Good point. I'll book it now before someone else does. || Woman: I'm sure that will help. || Man: I hope so. It's better to sort it out early.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you booked anywhere for our weekend away?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I found a guest house near the beach, but it only has one room left.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Is it expensive?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not too bad. The problem is breakfast isn't included.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's fine. I'd rather stay close to the sea than save a few pounds and have a long walk every morning.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good point. I'll book it now before someone else does.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I'm sure that will help.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I hope so. It's better to sort it out early.

questions.question: What is most important to the woman?
questions.explanation: She says she would rather stay close to the sea than save money.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling a friend about a college course.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How's your computer course? || Man: Useful, but different from what I expected. I thought we'd learn how to design websites, but it's mostly about spreadsheets. || Woman: Are you disappointed? || Man: A little, though the teacher is excellent. [9]I just wish the course description had been clearer before I paid for it[/9]. || Woman: Could you change to another class? || Man: Maybe next term. For now, I'll continue. || Woman: That sounds fair. || Man: Yes, and I think everyone will understand the situation.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How's your computer course?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Useful, but different from what I expected. I thought we'd learn how to design websites, but it's mostly about spreadsheets.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you disappointed?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: A little, though the teacher is excellent. I just wish the course description had been clearer before I paid for it.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Could you change to another class?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Maybe next term. For now, I'll continue.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds fair.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, and I think everyone will understand the situation.

questions.question: What does the man say about the course?

options.C: The information about it was misleading.

questions.explanation: He says the course description should have been clearer before he paid.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a new café.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you been to the café by the library? || Woman: Yes. The cakes looked amazing, but I only had coffee. || Man: Was it good? || Woman: [10]Very good, and the staff were really friendly, which made me want to go back[/10]. || Man: I thought the chairs were uncomfortable. || Woman: I noticed that, but I didn't stay long. For a quick drink, it's a nice place. || Man: I hadn't thought of that. || Woman: Neither had I at first, but now it seems obvious.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you been to the café by the library?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. The cakes looked amazing, but I only had coffee.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Was it good?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Very good, and the staff were really friendly, which made me want to go back.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I thought the chairs were uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I noticed that, but I didn't stay long. For a quick drink, it's a nice place.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I hadn't thought of that.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Neither had I at first, but now it seems obvious.

questions.question: What did the woman like about the café?
questions.explanation: She says the staff were really friendly, which made her want to return.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a lost bag.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I realised my bag was missing when I got home from town. At first I thought I had left it in the bookshop, because I had put it down there while paying. Then I remembered carrying it onto the bus. [11]The driver later found it under a seat and handed it in at the bus station[/11]. Nothing was missing, which was a huge relief because my keys and student card were inside.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I realised my bag was missing when I got home from town. At first I thought I had left it in the bookshop, because I had put it down there while paying. Then I remembered carrying it onto the bus. The driver later found it under a seat and handed it in at the bus station. Nothing was missing, which was a huge relief because my keys and student card were inside.

questions.question: Where was the woman's bag found?
questions.explanation: She says the driver found the bag under a seat on the bus.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a local park.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you seen the plans for the park? || Woman: Yes. A new play area and better paths sound good. || Man: Some people don't want the old trees removed. || Woman: I understand that. [12]What worries me is the café building. If it's too big, the park may feel less peaceful[/12]. || Man: True. The quiet is why many people go there. || Woman: Exactly. Improvements are fine, but not if they change the place completely. || Man: That must be useful. || Woman: It is, especially when there are several things to think about.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you seen the plans for the park?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. A new play area and better paths sound good.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Some people don't want the old trees removed.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I understand that. What worries me is the café building. If it's too big, the park may feel less peaceful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True. The quiet is why many people go there.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. Improvements are fine, but not if they change the place completely.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That must be useful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is, especially when there are several things to think about.

questions.question: What is the woman concerned about?

options.A: the safety of the play area
options.C: the size of a new building

questions.explanation: She says she is worried that the café building may be too big.

extracts.context: You will hear a boy telling a friend about a school project.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: Have you finished your history project? || Boy: Almost. I worked with Sam, and we shared the research fairly. || Girl: Are you presenting it tomorrow? || Boy: Yes. [13]I'm happy with the information, but I wish we'd practised speaking together because our timing may be a bit messy[/13]. || Girl: You still have tonight. || Boy: I know. I'll call him after dinner and practise once more. || Girl: That sounds sensible. || Boy: Yes, I think it will make things much easier next time.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Have you finished your history project?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Almost. I worked with Sam, and we shared the research fairly.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Are you presenting it tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes. I'm happy with the information, but I wish we'd practised speaking together because our timing may be a bit messy.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: You still have tonight.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I know. I'll call him after dinner and practise once more.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: That sounds sensible.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes, I think it will make things much easier next time.

questions.question: What is the boy worried about?
questions.explanation: He says their timing may be messy because they have not practised speaking together.
